channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. — 25 replies — 98.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo.

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-06-11 16:27 UTC

OP

So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. How the fuck is it legal for them to destroy so much shareholder value? I WANT MY FUCKING MONEY BACK NOW!!

Early replies (first 3)

62348099
>>62348037
You did not lose anything, shoe shine boy. change your diapers and calm the FUCK down. Because you didn't sell yet. Right?

62348149
Better price to buy m8. You don't believe in the business anymore or somethin

62348170
If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead, I don't know what to tell you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62348177
>>62348037
nintendo is dead. the new pokemon games all have a super weird art style

62348263
>>62348170
I couldn't tell, because I stopped paying attention (and let it die i guess)

Is there anything these days like Minecraft Halloween update, the summer of dayz, or starceaft brood war Use Map Settings maps?

62348278
>>62348037

Are you the same retard that asked about buying them after an entire thread told you they were cooked? I remember giving clear examples of why this company is dogshit, specifically not owning Pokémon 100%. They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son. Sounds like you are a braindead tendie lie the rest.

62349809
>>62348099
I would have lost more if I hadn't sold, retard.

62349911
>>62348037
I FUCKING told end of last year that Nintendo was in a bubble. Yes, it's in the archives, likely with either Xenoblade pics or Rosalina pics.
>Muh P/E is useless
It isn't. As many retards will soon learn again for lot of shit they buy at overpriced prices.
Now it's at a more reasonable price, still elevated, but low enough that I'm regularly buying.

62349918
>>62349911
nintebdo movies will each make a bil

62349956
the next ubisoft

62349957
>>62348037
you could have made 100k scalping pokemon cards, wtf are you doing OP

62349964
>>62348037
Japan as a society is super conservative and stuck in its way to a retarded degree, what do you expect from the land of fax machines and archipelago syndrome?

62350244
>>62349911
What stocks options are you buying bro?

62350352
>>62348037
>all good games are made by indie developers
why would you bet on any of the big companies? they are creatively bankrupt bloated bureaucracies

62350621
Thanks for piquing my interest. Now I am off to do some research. What exactly is it that they are doing to piss you off? Other than losing sole rights to Pokémon cards?

62350665
>>62348037
I tried to tell you.

No, literally, I tried to. I was the one /v/ fag screaming at you that nintendo was on its way down the same path that killed ubisoft and ruined video games.

62350696
>>62348037
slurp the dip

62350697
>>62348037
>actually investing real money in Bing Bing wahoo

62350703
>>62350665
I am not too familiar with the video game industry. I considered to buy Ubisoft then decided not to a while ago. Had other things that ended up going for instead. Maybe I should wait out the tech bubble popping. But I do love pre-dumped stocks.

62350739
Zooming out this just looks like it is at the 2022-3 lows. The bull run happened last year. Investing in an economy in another country. Their bull runs will often have a different timing. However this can clearly go down a lot more.

62350816
>>62348037
Did you use the Switch 2 for a bunch of hours? Yes? So you saw a device with a 2-hour battery life and no games, but you still decided to go all-in on this company?

62350869
Nintendo used to put out sub par hardware that still managed to be hugely successful in the console wars, they had the handheld market cornered, 3ds was kino. How the decision to kill and eat Gameboy with a retarded tablet console got through will always be perplexing to me.

62350907
>>62350816
Ouch. Released in 2025. That caused it to dump. No wonder that bull run was so short. Nintendo bull runs usually last over a year and don't go down that fast. I think this company is recoverable but I do not know what they plan on doing to make better products. Will this be like HP and Ubisoft being shit and making bad decisions until they get a new CEO and clean house? Scratch that. Forgot what was going on at Ubisoft that made me 2nd guess buying.

62351015
Oh now I know why I didn't invest in Ubisoft is because the different ticker types were too confusing. That on top of shady incompetence going on right now.

62351629
stop buying with emotions and hopes
rape these companies like they would rape you

gaming companies at release you always short. even TTWO

item.title: spaceX IPO — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
spaceX IPO

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-11 16:40 UTC

OP

are we ready for the biggest scam in history of histories?

ELON MUSK, YOU FAGGGOT

when will your ponzi scheme finally collapse, you fucking hobo

Early replies (first 3)

62351469
>>62351461
like 8 different brokers and crypto apps have shilled this shit to me fucking lmao

are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?

62351500
It's actually going to be a repeat of the ICP ico isn't it?

62351509
>>62351469
>are they gonna rape retail instantly or let it pump for a few days before the raping?
supposedly this is entering indexes in 15 days

total scam of scams. can't believe this is fucking happening. everyone (ameriburgers) about to get raped

i'm not american but I would've voted for the faggot. but this...
it would've either been: continuation of woke lunacy or scams

hard times
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AEB6ibtdPZc

>are they gonna
the cult is real. it will pump and potentially crater with ai bust in 6-12 months (or god knows when or if)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62351513
SpaceX IPO is literally the end of the stock market in both name and theory for the rest of all human history

62351521
>>62351461
Gaps to 300 on the first day, easily

62351530
>>62351461
21 hours till elon becomes worlds first trillionaire

62351534
>>62351461
I will post my gains here tomorrow.

62351545
>>62351461
>>62351500
>>62351513
Thanks bros, I just sold 10k off my portfolio to buy in :)

62351548
>>62351461
>Elmo Elongated Muskrat company bad
You do know that reddit has a stock board and they'll even give you reddit gold for saying such brave things?

62351550
>>62351509
>(or god knows when or if)
ai bust is going to happen

62351553
>>62351521
Slippage is gonna be insane on second one. I don’t see SPCX pricing below 200 after the first minute. Be sure to have a sizeable lump of cash if you are YOLOing.

62351559
>>62351548
>>Elmo Elongated Muskrat company bad
log off this board please

reddit and all liberal faggots were glazing this guy until he went Trumpster

MAGA BABY

62351572
>>62351530
whats even the point, the stupid nigger just works 24/7 and is CEO of 5 companies for fun or something who knows

like go spend your trillion dollars with those 10 kids and help them with their homework and take them on trips with the Private jet nigga wtf?? buy some 600M dollar mega yacht and have the kids go crazy while crossing the Atlantic on a mega mansion boat. Maybe find a nice woman your age to date and spend time with her too

62351576
>>62351548
about the only good thing this faggot did, was to put all the liberal dollars he received to semi decent use. compared to the lunacy on the other side
other than that, he's a mega shameful-autist scammer

62351581
>>62351572
He wants to build an army + his own country. He wants to liberate us from our oppressive governments. Poorfags like you wouldn’t understand

62351589
>>62351581
i guess bro

62351702
Surely it will go up for a brief moment once trading opens right?

item.title: How will this affect South Koreas economy? — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How will this affect South Koreas economy?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-11 16:41 UTC

OP

How will this affect South Koreas economy?

Should I go all-in on a SK index fund?

Early replies (first 3)

62350030
>>62349989
I like that he recognizes bald suffering, but the issue is mostly rampant feminism, so effectively, he is an enabler.

62350349
>>62349989
by shaving you no longer have to worry about styling your hair and it's a repellent for nasty and bad women

62350516
>>62350349
>just bend over and let the norwood reaper fuck you in the ass

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350557
in the usa you should be able to pay for finasteride and minoxidil with an HSA. hair is protection from skin cancer.

62350562
>>62350349
You dont look like a 90s skinhead, you skinnyfat onions baldy.

62350565
>>62350557
Almost everywhere in the world generic finasteride options cost like $20-30 for an entire years supply.

Are the pharma companies scamming burgers by overcharging finasteride like they do with most other meds?

62350592
Baldy, baldy over there...

62350594
>>62350565
Except fin is a tranny drug poison. Post finasteride syndrome reddit has 15k unique weekly visits.

62350624
>>62350594
About 20% of men between 18-35 (who arent on fin) develop ED.

When people who do take fin develop ED, of course the drug that nukes users dicks (temporarily) 1% of the time is what they'll blame it on, while in reality it's something else entirely. I'd bet the number of finasteride users who develop permanent ED is pretty close to 20%, the same percentage as men who don't use it.

62350707
>>62349989
Just make it socially acceptable to wear hats everywhere. I am balding and I just wear a hat all the time and I was still able to get laid. Basically by the time I make it to the point the hat comes off it is too late and the sex happens anyway. I'd imagine this is what it is like when trans women trick straight men into bed with them.

62350754
>>62350707

62350920
>>62349989
attractive men = baby boom

62351695
mandatory plastic surgery
everyone looks like kpop
big orgy ensues

62351707
>>62350594
Finasteride has a half life of 6 hours and PFS is a fake condition invented by American grifters and bald cucks.

item.title: FUCK THESE STUPID FUCKING ROCKS — 14 replies — 40.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
FUCK THESE STUPID FUCKING ROCKS

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-11 16:21 UTC

OP

FUCK THESE STUPID FUCKING ROCKS

Early replies (first 3)

62349745
>>62349741
Reactionaries lose, so being a reactionary. Simple as.
Or don't, idgaf what you do.

62349747
>>62349741
why not just use your gold for something didn't you guys claim you can build computer parts with it?

62349748
>>62349747
Nigga a v2 rocket needs 40 tonnes of godl!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349783
>>62349741
Top buyers should go for spacex now.

62349788
>>62349741
Careful, they're bendy rocks :)

62351391
>>62349745
Good little bag holder! Glad I sold 10oz of gold last month. I'll let you hold onto it for me and pick it back up when it's $2k per ounce.

62351402
>>62349741
Expect gold to crash further. Iran just declared they will no longer allow any ships to pass through the strait of Hormuz, regardless of whether they pay a toll or not. So, interest rates are about to go higher, and cash-strapped commodity importers are about to liquidate gold reserves to stay solvent.

62351447
>>62351402
Is 2000 possible?

62351450
>>62351391
>back up when it's $2k per ounce.

In all seriousness, is this possible?

62351487
>>62351450
Is the USD still being printed to pay for the interest debt for printing more USD?

62351488
>>62351487
Please give me a yes or no answer

62351517
>>62351450
No.
Are you panicking? we won't even go below 3.5k.

62351543
>>62351517
It needs to gp back to 2000 so I can buy

62351604
>>62349741
>He literally bought shiny rocks

item.title: ETF Recommendations — 28 replies — 33.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ETF Recommendations

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-06-11 15:51 UTC

OP

Is it too late to buy some sector specific ETFs to try and ride the wave of this bull market
Obviously no one really knows what will happen, just wish I could have been investing a few years sooner
I already have a large standing in VOO but want to chase growth via sectors
My assumption was to not go into semiconductors or anything based on the computing of AI but things like energy that deal with the infrastructure of AI

Early replies (first 3)

62345926
I've rejected at least 3 east asian women. they're nice girls or whatever but they're not attractive. I exclusively am a latina/brown woman guy, i'm sorry.

62345956
>>62345926
Based. I like non inbred-looking women with a bit of meat on them too.

62345961
>>62345819
>this bull market
lol you are about to get heemed

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62345983
>>62345961
why

62345996
I'm dating a half korean half south american, she's cute, low body count, people pleases the shit out of me (full body massage almost every time I see her) Amazing sex and we're like best friends. Problem is, I want white kids... or I could just be companions with her for the rest of my life and be childless. So I have been going through a crisis in figuring out what to do. Thanks for reading my blog, feel free to roast me or give an opinion

62346078
>>62345926
>>62345996
larp

62346171
>>62345996
my uncle married a chinese woman, they had 2 hapas togethe, the wife was pretty weird and extremely overprotective of the kids (e.g. she wouldn't let them play alone in their fully fenced back yard, even when they were 10+ years old).
They got divorced a few years ago and my aunt moved back to china with her daughter, and my cousin now hates his dad and has trooned out and posts pictures of him wearing dresses on instagram.

62346192
>>62345996
Just have the kids with her. My wife and I are pure white and your mutt daughter can pop out my son's 3/4 white kids on the side while he takes a break from his pure white wife if needed to help restore your bloodline.

62346478
>>62346192
lmao
>>62346171
this is pretty much my nightmare scenario
>>62346078
have sex incel asian girls are easy to colonize

62346501
>>62346478
what part of what i said even sounds like being an incel lol

62346508
>>62345996
Fuck, I have the same dichotomy, I'd love white kids, either blonde or brunette (I have dark hair, grreen eyes, part of my family has blonde hair and green/blue eyes). I want to date and perhaps marry an asian but something about my children potentially not having bright eyes pushes me away.

62346587
>>62346501
not believing random anons could have an asian gf, sounds like projection or defensiveness.
>>62346508
basically this

62346703
>>62346587
ok anyway op

62346750
>>62345819
ETFs die this year unless highly specialized. VOO and QQQ are going to get killed or oroboros churn from further concentrations. Invest in bottlenecks like tungsten.

62346790
>>62346750
but why exactly can you explain

62348362
If you aren't playing SOXS/SOXL you are losing, and with PDT gone you can play both ways.

62348734
>>62346508
>potentially
lol

62349415
>>62346508
asian maid
blonde wife

62349524
>>62345819
I don't think it's too late. I have around 20% of my portfolio in semiconductors and healthcare sectors with decend profits. Soon space innovation sector might open so I'll slurp that too. Besides that I have no interest in other sectors like defence, consumer goods, fintech etc....

62349557
>>62345996
There is nothing wrong with hapa daughters. All the most popular Kpop starlets are colonies.

62350599
>>62349557
>colonies
?

62350606
>>62350599
Colonies are the result of something being colonized

62350616
>>62349557
The only famous hapa kpop idol is Danielle and her career is fucked. Don't bring up Somi or Vernon cause they aren't famous

62350640
>>62346508
>potentially
You need to accept that if you have kids with an Asian woman they’ll be Asian. They will not look white. They will not “be” white. They’ll be a constant reminder of that. They will not look like you basically at all. Is that what you want? I am white and I have one kid so far and my kid looks like me.
>Blonde hair
>Blue eyes
I could not imagine having it any other way.

62350661
>>62345996
A child that is 1/4 Korean, 1/4 South American and 1/2 white will come out looking like a South American "white."

62350862
>>62345996
marry and have kids with her you stupid retard

62351184
>>62350640
>>62350862
These are the two conflicting arguments I have with myself every day

62351458
>>62351184
maybe should have thought considered that before you thirsted for asian pussy you stupid fuck

item.title: A 30 y from 2022 with a face value of $1000 would roughly have a market value of $500 today. — 22 replies — 18.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
A 30 y from 2022 with a face value of $1000 would roughly have a market value of $500 today.

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-11 15:32 UTC

OP

A 30 y from 2022 with a face value of $1000 would roughly have a market value of $500 today.
..and vice versa, should the yield go down, say we go back to 2% in 2030, then $1000 in a 5% bond would have a market value of ~$2000.

Say nothing happens, or the yield surge further.. You are still getting that 5% ROI on the face value, you don't have to sell, you can just enjoy a stable cashflow.

Early replies (first 3)

62349850
not buying your worthless dollars, mr. Bessent, you literal faggot.

62349858
I think this is a good investment, because from my perspective bond yields will go lower.
1) None of the inflation seems to be structural in any way, it's fuelled by active decisions - tariffs, Hormuz, widening of the budget deficit.
2) The economy really is bifurcated - this includes the labour market, and I think that a need will rise to adjust monetary policy to aid the lagging part of the economy, support the labour market.
3) An extension of 2, if the AI bubble pop, rates will go lower. You cut rates during a recession to stimulate the economy.
4) back to the 5% ROI.. If none of the above materialize, you are just stuck at a suboptimal investment, but 5% is not bad, especially because it is risk free.

62349906
I knew some dipshit that went all in on bonds in I think early 2023 because he thought stocks were topping and interest rates just had to go lower. Missed out on a lot of gains, but admittedly, the yield curve was inverted at the time.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349916
rates will go higher this year, the impending oil crisis will see to that
don't buy long-term bonds until they hit at least 6%

62349921
>>62349858
>it's fuelled by active decisions
Yeah, surely it has nothing to do with compounding debt where the interest payments alone will soon make up the #1 largest expenditure and is very obviously spiraling out of control and everyone in charge are sticking their heads in the sand, lmao

62349954
>>62349921
Japan has a humongous debt, and it's interest rate is sub 1%.
..because what else could it be? no way they could ever make interest payment of 5%.
The US will probably follow the same track, cut interest rate, force retirement funds to buy these shit-yielding-bonds.

62349961
>>62349916
This is my thinking as well, i think 5% is interesting.. But we might get to 6-7%, think i will pull the trigger at these levels, close enough to the "10% average of the S&P", without any of the risk, and with the potential upside of x2.

62349974
>>62349961
with the added upside that treasuries are exempt from all but federal taxes

62349977
>>62349840
>say we go back to 2% in 2030
not gonna happen so no point in saying it

62349983
>>62349961
wouldn't go all in even at 6%, inflation will get really ugly in the next 30 years, you're better off diversifying

62350006
>>62349983
people hate inflation more than unemployment, that was made clear by the voters globally after COVID
I think we'll get a Volcker Shock 2.0 and then budget austerity well before inflation gets out of hand again

62350020
>>62349840
Trump is determined to increase inflation, therefore the bonds are sold because smart investors know, thus yields go up.

Hoping those shitty ass bonds will go up is the most hopium shit ass trade I ever heard.

62350103
>>62350006
Inflation is coming down, but yeah, we will probably see a couple more rate hikes for good measure..

62350133
>>62350103
Core CPI is the wrong measure here, the oil supply shock is the main inflation driver, and will be for the forseeable future

62350164
>>62349858
>rates will go lower
Doubt.

62350179
>>62350133
yeah, think the Hormuz impact on oil will only worsen from here. A lot of the pressure on oil has been alleviated by strategic reserves (world wide).
..and even if the strait "opens" within the next couple of months, the fallout will likely continue for the remainder of 2026.

However, the core CPI is important because it shows inflation isn't structural, but event driven, temporarily - if you will.

62350190
>>62350179
depends on your definition of "temporary"
even if we somehow avoid the hyperbolic price spike that could occur at any moment once the market collectively decides reserves are too low, oil prices will remain elevated for a while

I'm hearing that the general estimate is that we'll need a week of normal supply for every day the strait has been closed so far, which would be almost two years at this point, and it just gets longer with each passing day

62350217
I learned about bonds recently.

How the fuck are they considered a reliable and safe investment when a 1% interest rate over 20 years bond will result in 20% price decrease in your bond? it's basically a bet against inflation. There is no guarantee bonds won't be giving 8% interests one day. i guess it can go back down and prices will come back but if you bought bonds at 3.5% you're already fucked. bag holding for a 1% net profit after inflation each year. beyond cucked.

62350228
>>62350217
only the most idiotic of fools bought long-term bonds back during ZIRP, no sane investor would do such a thing

62350236
>>62350228
well, billions were sold so some people or institutions were buying? it was considered the giga safe play lmao

62350259
>>62350236
people really thought interest rates were going to be super low forever, pure insanity

62351359
>>62349954
>it's interest rate is sub 1%
Uh, not any more

item.title: Is using leverage for bitcoin possible/worth it for a long term cyclical investor? — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is using leverage for bitcoin possible/worth it for a long term cyclical investor?

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-11 16:29 UTC

OP

Is using leverage for bitcoin possible/worth it for a long term cyclical investor?

I’ve always thought the fees on perps would eat you up but there’s gotta be another way right? I’m european so CFDs? I’m not sure I trust Saylor enough to justify MSTR. I only want like 2-3x lev

Early replies (first 3)

62350042
>>62349504
If you are poor like me you can probably use a 0% credit card. Put all your spending, bills etc on the card and put your cashflow onto bitcoin.

Was able to get a £15k card 4-5 years back. I stuck it all into stocks instead of crypto at the time, but was still able to make a killing

62351642
>>62349504
>CFDs
dear lord just kill yourself now
[spoiler]don't [/spoiler]

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: >Iran completely shuts the strait >BTC goes up >Oil goes down Why is the market reacting positively to this news, when i… — 11 replies — 6.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Iran completely shuts the strait >BTC goes up >Oil goes down Why is the market reacting positively to this news, when i…

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-11 14:52 UTC

OP

>Iran completely shuts the strait
>BTC goes up
>Oil goes down
Why is the market reacting positively to this news, when it reacted negatively the first time this happened?

Early replies (first 3)

62349904
>>62349889
This is what broken markets do, the head of the Chicago CME warned about this. It's all fun and games for a while, until it breaks into a biblical catastrophe.
Which will happen end of summer.

62349914
The market is pricing in peace and a quick resolution to this small excursion.

62349976
>>62349904
We (yes, we) allowed a bunch of mentally ill hoarders, lolcows even, into power. Naturally, they made clown policies, which clown the markets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349979
>>62349976
Solution: Denying power. No more politburo commies, no more billionaires, no more politicians, behind seven layers of private scurity. Direct control and accountability for everyone.

62350027
>>62349979
well that will never happen

62350047
>>62349914

>Beginning of month 4 of quick resolution to this small excusion
>25 million dollar Apache helicopter destroyed by cheap drone with a lawnmower engine
>Hormuz is still closed

62350070
>>62350047
Americans deserve this for failing the emotional IQ test

62350079
>>62350070
*failing the emotional IQ test twice

62350618
>>62349889
Not really seeing the problem here tho

62350654
>>62349889
The market is like a woman, believing what they want to believe.

62351147
>>62349889
Hormuz is not an American problem. It's not even a goal of this war.

item.title: I Love Silver, BRICs Shills Like Peter Schiff are Wrong — 8 replies — 4.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
I Love Silver, BRICs Shills Like Peter Schiff are Wrong

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-11 14:56 UTC

OP

The worse thing about my journey buying silver are all the shills who talk about the "American Empire" and how China and Russia will rise up and replace it.

Not happening.

I'm Chinese. My partner's family is Chinese. Everyone we know wants to leave China. It's just as shit as the rest of the world right now, but it also lacks a lot of history due to communists destroying everything. It's not taking over anything any time soon.

Also, Iran is getting it's assed kicked. Iran is like some retard that is in a street fight with an MMA fighter. The MMA fighter has the retard pinned to the ground, telling him to relax and calm down, while the retard keeps yelling, saying he would kill the MMA fight if he let him stand up. Iran is the retard in this story.

I buy silver because it is objectively money. Solar panels are fake. AI is overhyped. BRICs is gay and is losing to Ukraine and Israel. Chinese video games suck. Stop giving gay reasons why silver should be valuable. Silver is valuable because it is a commodity that doesn't decay and is fungible. Aliens would recognize gold, silver, and platinum as money, not Bitcoin or fiat cash.

There is no good reason to short silver right now. Central banks are net buyers, and Indian/Arabs/Chinese. I look forward to the silver price in a few months.

INB4 "I'm not reading all of that"

Early replies (first 3)

62349720
>>62349655
Ok. But my 5kg of silver now has me at a loss.

62349723
>>62349720
That's because you're a reactionary, who bought a peek. Buy more as it goes down. Or don't, less demand for the shiny, means more shiny for me. I don't give a fuck.

62349725
>>62349723
I bought last year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349761
>>62349723
I wish I could like comments on 4chan, because then I would like yours.

All the negative sentiment on silver makes me think we're about to have a huge upswing. Trump is now bombing Iran, which will probably force them into a deal.

If they don't make a deal, the US will just keep bombing them. They already don't control the straight. Oil is getting through. This war is the biggest nothingburger of all time.

62349782
why is there so much posting of gold and silver now.

>>62349723
>dood
>cheapies
lol
Retard

62350748
>>62349725
Really? You bought last year when silver ranged from the low 30s to the low 70s, then managed to sit through the last 5 months of supra $75 silver and you 'lost' money?

62351148
>>62349782
>so much posting of gold and silver now
Because eventually the USD interest debt will pass tax revenue.
Crypto is a fiat derivative along with of course stocks and bonds. Eventually people will realize how much of a scam fiat currency is and will go all in on the asset class that has been money for the last 6,000+ years of recorded human history, precious metals.

62351162
>>62351148
Okay so more useless doomer idiot posts from some fag that has been posting the squeeze squoze shit for 10 years, gotcha.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 191 replies — 38.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 191 • Updated: 2026-06-11 11:46 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62338077
>>62337475
linkpool made us think we could back in the day, it was all a scam, we all had a running node setup and we were waiting to be whitelisted, what a fking joke

62340215
>>62336936
You're misrepresenting my point to argue something else. Why are you doing this? You get nothing out of it unless you're a paid shill or just bored.

62340223
>>62337475
We tried. It's a full time job requiring a bunch of resources on top of the profits being nebulous thus souring a lot of people out of running their node [on AWS]. This was back in 2019 so I don't know how robust and improved running nodes is nowadays.

62340231
>>62340215
I argued against your point that "Chainlink paying the nodes from their own team wallets" decouples the link price from the usage; and then you literally asked me to refute your point that Chainlink doesn't pay the nodes from the uncirculating supply, and is instead sending the Link directly to Binance.

What the fuck are you even doing here?

62340287
>>62340231
> BTC
> No new BTC enters the market except through miners' hands.
> This means they decide and influence the price of BTC.
> This couples the BTC price to the usage of the network because miners will not sell less than the costs of operations

> Link
> new Link enters the market through payments to node operators, payments to developers, and to deals with companies
> Link node operators are no longer the sole arbiters who decide when new Link enters the market
> To access services from the network, you don't need to pay in Link, and your payments made aren't immediately converted to Link, if at all. This decouples the link token from accurately reflecting network activity
> Furthermore CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements, Ex: China's response to the Trump tariffs

https://etherscan.io/tx/0x6e347aa07b74b2330b8f3f5a9da44305cba0bf7e40b416bcf00ba9922c365fdd
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x3355d51eafaead23e063aba8a099a712a11ecb26e937f53d0850cd4c5b33801e
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x2e3b0111c94d9d0102e5822ccd9dc5f395c5b207b39a466432c1d634c4354eb6

^Above transactions took place just an hour before the great crash of October


Your job is to refute my conclusion that the link token price is NOT tied to network usage. Refute these points. You are free to disregard the BTC comparison since those are points in my favor unless you can refute my points. Any thing other than directly addressing and showcasing flaws in my logic (and I better hear something better than the low hanging fruit of "Node operators have to sell Link for cost operations too!") and I will respond with "token not needed".

62340304
>>62340287
You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.
This is what I refuted.
I pointed this out to you many times but you keep pretending not to understand or something.

62340325
>>62340304
>You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.

token not sneeded

62340347
>>62340325
>>62340287
>CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements
This is horseshit by the way.
He has always sold on Fridays, pretty much around the same hour, and always within 3 to 4 month intervals.

Sometimes Link pumps right after (many times in fact), sometimes some shit happens right after. But his timing is fully transparent.

62340403
>>62340347
Anon, the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do in response to Trump's tariffs. For the uninitiated, Trump proposed a 100% tariff on China and China responded with crashing the market. You can dig into the archives to verify. Type in either "Trump", "China" "Binance", "Link" or whatever and date it to October.

62340410
Fuck you chainlinkies!

Sincerely,

The whole XRP community

62340414
>>62340403
>the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do
Those links are the transactions Sergey made on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.
Which has been his unlocking regimen since forever.

62340536
>>62340287
If (almost) no one uses the network, will the token price change or remain (almost) unchanged?

62340953
>>62340347
>But his timing is fully transparent.


classic boyscout bold faced lie

there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales other than them doing it 4 times per year. often it will be completely different months as years past.

didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol

62340971
>>62340953
>there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales
- always around 7% of total supply annually
- always on Fridays
- always within 3 to 4 month intervals

62342184
>>62340953
>didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol
Those are the transactions I posted that >>62340971 is trying to brush off as "part of the plan"

He's moving the goal post even when this discussion just bolsters my point about the link token price not accurately reflecting network usage. I initially wasn't thinking of the "routine token unlocks" so I'll give you my thanks.

62342243
>>62342184
You're claiming he unlocked right before the 10/10 crash to "capitalize on market movements".

The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval. Like he's done for years.

62342296
>>62342243
Oh no no no, he most certainly did unlock just before the crash. It was part of the panic and it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis.

62342402
>>62342296
>he most certainly did unlock just before the crash
Who said he didn't?

>it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis
It was luck BECAUSE he'd already been selling on a 3-4 month basis for years at that point.

62342424
Reminder you must only say positive things, otherwise you are a shill.

62342440
>>62342424
say something nice about chainlink

62342501
>>62313866
>>62313871
Glad to see some of us are still here... I didn't do the presale but t0 Binance buyer.
It's absolutely grim the state of this project.

62342517
>>62342243
>The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.

yeah they do it once a quarter
this particular one we're talking about just happened to be directly before one of the largest selloffs in industry history

>but duuuuuuuuude he always does it like that

no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months. so no it is not just like previous years.

62342559
>>62342517
>no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months
That's because he's unlocking every 3 to 4 months. Not in the same month every time.
He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.

62342602
>dumps straight on the most shady CEX are "unlocks"
peak doublespeak

62344923
>>62342602
All of Chainlink's tokens go through Binance, including node payments.
Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

62346060
>>62342559
>He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.


not sure why you keep repeating this as if this implies some transparency.

>duuuuuuuuuude just hang out on the blockchain every friday and you'll see it coming

its ok to concede they don't want to be transparent here. if they did they would just post a set schedule.

>>62344923
>Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

no one falls for this outside of the biggest retard boyscouts
where do the coins go?

62346092
>>62346060
Unlocking the same amount annually for years, within the same interval for years, and even around the same time of day for years implies transparency, yes.

>where do the coins go?
among others to the nodes

62346134
link blowz

62349168
Posting in the best LINK thread now. Some good convos. I hope we all make it.

62350386
>>62349168
its actually starting to reverbate with the muh influencers, they actuall read this thread, it's a good thing my goal is reached im happy

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 63 replies — 35.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-06-11 14:55 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62345600
>>62345100
You haven't heard of Babylon? Native BTC staking already exists there. DYOR anon.

62345604
>>62339510
The real challenge is whether August actually lets you buy lower. Markets have a habit of humbling everyone's timelines.

62345772
>>62345299
Is 4year cycle even real?

62345775
>>62345127
40 years from now

62345776
>>62339328
I sold a small amount at 110k last year, bought a car. Unironically I felt bad once it started pumping back to 120k. Now my car has holded it's value better than BTC.

62345783
>>62345776
Car price depreciated. Nothing special

62345787
>>62341032
I just want x1000 . Utility is nothing

62345800
>>62345600
centralised services doesn't really give me a good impression

62345806
When to sell?

62345827
>>62339328
>Btc
>Eth
>Hbar
>Neo
>Etc
>Kava
>Rose
>Inj

62346229
>>62344890
Yep. Make sure you ignore the fiat value. If it falls to zero, BTC is a new way of finance independent from the outdated monetary system. Be grateful you are part of the revolution. I too will soon enter BTC paradise. Any day now...

62346754
>>62345800
Anon, where did I say anything about centralized services? You're arguing against something I never said. Read up on their TBVs and maybe it'll click.

62346765
>>62339730
That's understandable. It's hard to judge past decisions based on outcomes we couldn't have known at the time.

62349025
>>62346229
Btc will never go to zero. Stop smoking

62349066
>>62345827
oldie bags but gold . Hodl

62349435
>>62346754
Onchain is the game

62349439
>>62345806
Depends on you lmao

62349795
>>62339328
If I had sold to pay off debt, buy a house, or improve my life, I wouldn't regret it. You can't eat unrealized gains.

62350085
>>62345787
go for shitcoins like memecoins on solana

62350154
>>62339328

Buy Polkadot

https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2025/03/establishment-of-the-strategic-bitcoin-reserve-and-united-states-digital-asset-stockpile/

62350283
>>62349435
Yeah. Going on-chain is one thing. Now let's see if the infrastructure can back it up. The public testnet on Aave V4 will speak for itself.

62350284
>>62350154
What happened to polkadot?

62350290
>>62345050

62350315
>>62350290
Ethereum is king

62351078
>>62350154
it's hurried from hell

62351090
>>62350283
Aave performs well as a DeFi platform, but the token itself is struggling.

62351106
>>62339883
Taking profits is never a mistake. Calling the end of crypto has historically been a much tougher bet.

62351114
>ETHEREUM

62351129
>>62345772
It's real until macro, ETFs, institutions, and changing market structure decide otherwise.

62351159
>>62339328
>Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move
It's the definition of a bottom.

item.title: Reminder that XRP bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever incr… — 16 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Reminder that XRP bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever incr…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-11 14:21 UTC

OP

Reminder that XRP bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever increase in value (they just say kaboom or accuse you of owning an equally shitty crypto)

Early replies (first 3)

62345876
>>62345842
KABOOOOOOOM YOU HOLD CHAINLINK OOOOOOOOOOOKOKKKKKKK

62345879
>>62345876
And lo it was as foretold

62345931
>>62345842
Reminder that <literally any cryptocuckshit> bagholders have absolutely nothing to say when questioned on why their token should or would ever increase in value (they just say kaboom or accuse you of owning an equally shitty crypto)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62345936
>>62345931
Fair. Monero or privacy crypto could have been world changing, except you can never cash out or buy anything with it

62345958
>>62345936
By design.

62345960
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62345970
most of the so called utility coins have been around for 14-15 years, if they were actually going to do something they would have 10 times already. Even the fucking dotcom boom crashed and recovered in half the time. What else is there to wait for? Clarity Act? Don't make me laugh.

62346026
>>62345970
You could have held Walmart and apple for 20 years before it really took off, 12 years for Amazon…so you’re a retard

62346045
>>62345960
Does nothing for the xrp price

62346051
>>62345960
>30+ partners

62347125
>>62346045
I’ve been holding XRP for years, but no single good news has pumped its prices. Even when XRP got clarity in court, its price only went up measly 40 cents before it dumped back down. You can declare XRP intergalactic bridge currency, but it will still dump or crab. This is because they’re using bots to suppress the price, which has been documented for a long time.

62349844
>>62347125
>hurr xrp doesn't pump on news and is suppressed
You're an idiot.
XRP did a 2x after the lawsuit ended, and Bitcoin even pumped to help it.

62350438
>>62349844
>You're an idiot
>BTC/USDT chart

62350641
Back then, at $3.20 in 2018
>xrp is going to go to a gorillion dollars because everything will have to use it!
Now, at $1.15
>um ackshually competition is healthy we never claimed we'd run world finance, it's no big deal that everywhere xrp shows up its in a list alongside a bunch of things we claim are ponzis, because we have no moat or exclusivity that matches our original retarded claims... surely it will go to a gorillion dollars anyway!

Lmfao

62350676
>>62345960
Listed alongside ave, solana, and lovable!
Now THATS what I call an xrp monopoly
BOOOOOOOOOOM

62350963
>>62350438
>and Bitcoin even pumped to help it

readin be hard n shiet yo

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 106 replies — 6.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 106 • Updated: 2026-06-11 00:40 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62329055
>>62309503
intrinsic value doesn't exist
bitcoin is going to dozens of millions but for now the end of the bear cycle is not over and the bottom will likely be end of year

62329158
>>62309515
Buy the fucking dip and stake the little you can, you can never catch the bottom. If you panic sell, it will start pumping.

62329178
I hope this thread can survive another week or two, so we can laugh at OP when Bitcoin dumps to $50k

62329324
>>62329055
There is no bear cycle, it is just retards fire selling so they have free cash to buy into SpaceX IPO and end up inevitably rugged.

The BUYROG will win in the end. I hope this thread stays up until the end od the month, when BTC is at 75k and trending upwards because S&P refused to steal people's retirement to buy the latest AI scam.

62329342
>>62329324
Man, I hope you're right
It's been 8 straight months of dumping, need to see some green for a change

62329345
>>62329324
>52% drop over the last 8 months, with lower and lower bottoms over time
>"There is no bear cycle"

62329476
>>62329342
Serious, dude, just ignore these people (bear posters are just /smg/ bots and /pmg/ twofaced assholes, go tend to your garden, and come back in a few weeks.

The last time it was the same shit. The BUYROG called the bottom, they all laughed and posted bears, then the bottom was kept, and BTC rallied to near 80k before the SpaceX IPO prepping started.

We are frankly better off without these people. BTC is literally outperforming silver and 90% of crypto right now, yet they act like this is the end of crypto (it is not).

The idea of crypto is what matters, and they hate and fear this idea because they will lose, as they have lost many times before. Just zoom out the price charts.

This is not buy x sell, it is good x evil, and we are better off without retards buying into BTC so they can pump and dump.

62329478
>>62309503
/biz/ tricked me into waiting for a lower dip during the last bottom. I'm going to DCA now

62329970
>>62309503
Dunno m8 at my local groxery store they dib't accept bitcoin. Where xan you buy groceries, other than your cherrypicked example?

62331787
>>62329478
/biz/ always tells you to buy the dip. Then the dip comes, and they tell you not to buy it.

62331939
Brought to you by Epstein and Co.: Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value!

62333686
>>62309503
I bought the rally (my first cycle) and the dips/bottom. waiting on the good days now

62333871
>>62309503
30k

62334072
63K REJECTED

62334365
>>62309503
>65K is the bottom
But it went lower anon?

62334381
>>62329970
you can buy anything you want with a coinbase card, what're you on about geez?

62336135
THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING AT ALL OF THE BEAR SHILLS

YOU LOST. YOU SOLD THE BOTTOM

THE BUYROG WINS AGAIN

BEARSBANE

62336205
>>62309503
At least until it dips again. 26K is the real bottom, screen cap this.

62336221
>>62336205
It is never going 55k. I have no idea why PM metal shills (most of the bear is PM fags, we tested it) watch silver get ended by the Fed, and decide to lash out trolling people's financial lives.

I am glad the BUYROG convinced some people to not miss yet another dip.

62336238
>>62336135
>THE BUYROG IS LAUGHING
>still below the 65k "bottom" of OP

62337880
>>62336238
YOU LOST

62340948
Bear bros, our response? The buyrog keeps calling it right.

62341211
>>62340948
Sorry, I can’t hear anything with the noise of all these coins crashing at the same time.

62341507
>>62341211
just bought $20k MSTY

62342143
Your conviction is admirable, but not warranted.

62344662
Bobobros, it’s not really crashing anymore…

62344689
>>62309503
Bitcoin brought about crypto, but it has limited growth potential at this point due to both mcap and being so centralized. Decentralized coins will keep growing, and some have grown even while everything else tanks, like Nerva/XNV.

62344772
>>62344689
Explain how btc is centralized.

62348813
>>62344772
You need a centralized exchange to buy and sell your digital pixels so the government can tax and keep track of your transactions.

62348845
>>62348813
Yes, you simply can't buy it with cash.

item.title: XRP **EMERGENCY** UPDATE — 2 replies — 5.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP **EMERGENCY** UPDATE

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-11 16:21 UTC

OP

Some breaking xrp news

Early replies (first 3)

62351536
Remember when Brad said he didnt want to support the clarity act?

62351601
>>62351406
Why do you keep making these corny linkie threads to cope with objectively losing?
>>62351536
He literally never said that. He said he would support a bill which served crypto and would oppose one which didn't in the very early days of the formulation when the Clarity Act was leaning a bad direction. He has literally posted to mark up and pass it repeatedly and been lobbying for its passage on mainstream news on Fox Business. Why would you choose to be so low IQ and frustrated rather than change your thesis based on what's true? INSANE cope.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.
